name: Leek and Bacon Roulade
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a skillet, cook the bacon over medium heat, stirring occasionally until it is just crisp. Transfer the cooked bacon to a paper towel-lined plate and discard all but 1 tablespoon of the fat.
3. Cook the leeks in the remaining fat and butter over medium-low heat, stirring until they are softened. Stir in the cream and bring the liquid to a boil.
4. Simmer the mixture, stirring occasionally, until it is thickened and the cream is absorbed.
5. Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the Parmesan, caraway seeds, parsley, Dijon mustard, remaining bacon, salt, and pepper to taste.
6. Spread the filling evenly over the warm egg sponge, leaving a 1-inch border all around. Roll the roulade jelly-roll fashion, beginning with a long side.
7. Trim the ends on a diagonal. Using the aid of the towel and wax paper, transfer the roulade, seam side down, to an ovenproof platter. Spread gently with the softened butter and sprinkle with the Parmesan.
8. Bake the roulade for 10 minutes, or until heated through and the Parmesan is melted.
Yield: 8 first course servings
